title: Avoid Unexpected Behavior from Printf-Style Format Strings
description: Printf-style format strings are interpreted at runtime, not validated by the Java compiler. This can lead to unexpected behavior or runtime errors. Ensure the good behavior of printf-style formats when calling the format methods of several classes.
recommendation: Ensure that printf-style format strings are correctly written and tested to prevent unexpected behavior at runtime. Be especially careful when using format methods of classes like java.util.Formatter, java.lang.String, java.io.PrintStream, MessageFormat, and java.io.PrintWriter.
impact: Failure to follow this rule can lead to unexpected behavior or even runtime errors in your code, potentially leading to security vulnerabilities or software crashes.

checks.message: Ensure that printf-style format strings are correctly written and tested to prevent unexpected behavior at runtime.
